requirements.title: Scenario Model
requirements.acceptance: Scenario data expresses unclear-requirements path, current step, choices, terminal options, and source refs.

requirements.title: Scenario Slice Navigation
requirements.acceptance: Users see focused active scenario and explicit transition points.

requirements.title: Stepwise Command Choice
requirements.acceptance: Users follow command, condition, next choices, and closing node.

requirements.title: Continuation Route Panel
requirements.acceptance: Closing nodes show stop, quality, and knowledge routes in detail panel.

requirements.title: Upstream Citation Layer
requirements.acceptance: Command facts align with maestro-flow and support citation status.

requirements.title: Validation Checklist
requirements.acceptance: Internal users can self-check first command, next command, and stop/continue choices.

constraints.constraint: Scenario data must live outside React rendering components.
constraints.rationale: Static inline arrays block scalable scenario slices.

constraints.constraint: Secondary routes and source badges belong in the detail panel.
constraints.rationale: Avoids choice overload while preserving continuation choices.

constraints.constraint: Command facts must align with maestro-flow and support SourceRef metadata.
constraints.rationale: Submodule is command-chain source of truth.

domain.problem_statement: Users need to choose Maestro commands step by step from scenario state.
domain.audience: Internal validators and future Maestro users learning command sequences.

terminology.definition: Data model for scenario slices, steps, choices, terminal options, transitions, and citations.
Focused workflow subset for one user scenario.
Optional route from closing node into stop, quality, or knowledge capture.

insights.summary: Minimal state includes activeScenarioId, activeStepId, selectedNodeId, and optional visitedStepIds.

open_questions.question: Which exact maestro-flow files should cite the first scenario command sequence?
